summary.en: At least one substantial customer segment continuously benefits from a free-of-charge offer; another part of the business or another customer segment covers the cost. The book identifies three structurally distinct flavors: ad-supported, freemium, and bait-and-hook (lock-in).
summary.zh: 至少有一类客户群持续从「免费」中获益,而成本由业务的另一部分或另一类客户群承担。书中识别出三种结构上各不相同的子类型:广告支持、Freemium 增值订阅、诱饵与钩子(锁定)。

sources.label: Chris Anderson · Free: The Future of a Radical Price · Hyperion · 2009
Chris Anderson · 'Why $0.00 Is the Future of Business' · Wired · February 2008
Osterwalder & Pigneur · Business Model Generation · Wiley · 2010 (Pattern No. 4, pp. 88-107)

references.label: Chris Anderson · 'Why $0.00 Is the Future of Business' · Wired

note.en: Wired feature that named the pattern. Argues falling marginal cost of digital goods makes 'free' a serious business strategy, not a marketing gimmick.
note.zh: 把这一模式命名出来的 Wired 特稿。论证数字商品边际成本不断下降时,「免费」是一种严肃的商业战略,而非营销噱头。

references.label: Chris Anderson · Free: The Future of a Radical Price · Hyperion

note.en: Book-length expansion. Coined the three flavors used in the BMC pattern catalog: cross-subsidies (ad-supported), freemium, and gift economies. Names the freemium 1:9 / 1:99 ratios that became canonical SaaS thinking.
note.zh: 书籍版的完整扩展。明确划分了三种「免费」形态:跨边补贴(广告支持)、Freemium、礼物经济。书中提出的 1:9 / 1:99 付费转化比成为后来 SaaS 行业的标准思维。

references.label: Osterwalder & Pigneur · Business Model Generation · Wiley

note.en: Adapts Free into the BMC pattern catalog (Pattern No. 4). Splits the pattern into three structurally distinct sub-types — each gets its own paradigm case (Metro newspaper / Skype / Gillette razors).
note.zh: 把免费模式收录为 BMC 模式手册(第 4 号模式)。把这一模式拆为三个结构上不同的子类型 —— 各自对应一个范式案例(Metro 报纸 / Skype / 吉列剃须刀)。

summary.en: Free for one customer side because another side (advertisers) pays for their attention. Mechanically a multi-sided platform with one side priced at zero — the BMC overlaps strongly with MSP.
summary.zh: 其中一边客户免费,因为另一边(广告主)在为他们的注意力买单。机制上是「一侧定价为零」的多边平台 —— BMC 与多边平台模式高度重叠。

summary.en: Most users use a free tier; a small percentage (typically 5-10%) upgrades to a paid premium tier whose revenue covers the entire base. Cross-subsidy is across users on the same platform.
summary.zh: 绝大多数用户用免费版;一小部分(通常 5-10%)升级到付费高级版,这部分收入支撑整个用户基数。补贴发生在同一平台不同用户之间。

summary.en: An initial product is sold cheaply (or given away — the bait) to lock the customer into expensive recurring consumables (the hook). Cross-subsidy is intertemporal — cheap now, expensive later.
summary.zh: 前端产品低价出售或赠送(诱饵),把客户锁进高价的耗材或持续消费(钩子)。补贴发生在时间维度 —— 现在便宜,以后贵。
